source.author: SSA Catalog
source.notes: Yogurt + citrus chicken shawarma marinade with classic warm spices, designed for thighs or breast.

ingredients.label: Lemon zest
notes: About 1 tablespoon; optional but brightens flavor.

ingredients.label: Onion powder
notes: Sub: 60 g grated onion (about 1/2 small onion) for a more traditional profile.

ingredients.label: Ground turmeric
notes: About 1 teaspoon; classic shawarma color and earthiness.

ingredients.label: Ground cinnamon
notes: About 1/4 teaspoon; adds warm shawarma note.

ingredients.label: Ground cardamom
notes: About 1/4 teaspoon; optional but very authentic.

ingredients.label: Kosher salt
notes: About 1 3/4 teaspoons. This marinade is sized for ~1.4–1.8 kg (3–4 lb) chicken.
If you plan to salt again before cooking, reduce to 7 g.

ingredients.label: Mild chili powder (or cayenne for heat)
notes: For spicier: use 0.5–1 g cayenne instead.

ingredients.label: White vinegar
notes: Optional; adds extra tang. Skip if you prefer softer acidity.

steps.title: Make the marinade
instructions: In a bowl, whisk yogurt, olive oil, lemon juice, optional lemon zest, garlic, and optional vinegar until smooth.
Whisk in spices (cumin, coriander, paprika, turmeric, allspice, cinnamon, optional cardamom), black pepper, salt, and chili powder/cayenne.

steps.title: Coat the chicken
instructions: Add chicken (thighs preferred, or breasts) to a zip bag or covered container.
Pour marinade over chicken and massage until evenly coated.

steps.title: Marinate time
instructions: Avoid marinating much beyond 24 hours (yogurt + acid can soften texture too much).

steps.title: Cooking guidance (notes)
instructions: Shake off excess marinade before cooking to improve browning (leave a thin coating).
Cook hot: grill, cast iron skillet, or high-heat oven/roast.
Rest cooked chicken 5–10 minutes, then slice for wraps and bowls.

steps.title: Food safety
instructions: Marinate chicken in the refrigerator (not at room temperature).
Discard used marinade that touched raw chicken, or boil thoroughly before using for basting.
